value: Skipping the morning gym routine (d1) correlates with being sharper but less patient. User should not be surprised by this pattern.
Missing the gym correlates with decreased sharpness and patience. Consistent exercise has a noticeable positive impact on mood.
Noticed being sharper and less patient on days when gym session is missed. Gym routine correlates with calmness and patience.
Missing the gym routine leads to feeling sharper but also less patient.

User continues to observe negative cognitive and emotional impact (sharper but less patient) when skipping the non-negotiable morning gym. Reinforcing that rescheduling later rarely works. May need strategy to protect 4:45 gym slot even during heavy column draft days.
Skipping the morning gym makes him sharper and less patient. Trying to shift the gym to *after* morning writing usually means it doesn't happen.
